description: Figure 13 presents Charpy impact absorbed energy data for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels as functions of alloying element content, comprising two sub-panels. Panel (a) displays the impact energy of Fe-xMn-2Al-0.45C-0.05Si steel with Mn content ranging 17–22 wt.% tested at 0 °C and -196 °C. The upper curve (0 °C, triangle markers) shows high impact energy (~225–260 J) increasing slightly then plateauing, while the lower curve (-196 °C, inverted triangle markers) exhibits lower values (~45–145 J) that visually increase with Mn content. Panel (b) illustrates impact energy versus Al content (3–8 wt.%) for Fe-18Mn-xAl-0.6C steel, showing a sluggish increase from ~120 J to ~135 J as Al increases from 3 to 5 wt.%, followed by a steep decrease to ~15 J at 8 wt.% Al (square markers). Citation reports specific cryogenic values for panel (a): 80.5 J (17Mn), 49.8 J (19Mn), and 41.1 J (22Mn), suggesting a decreasing trend with Mn addition, and for panel (b): 122 J (3Al) increasing to 138 J (5Al) before dropping at 8Al. These results demonstrate critical composition-dependent cryogenic toughness behavior in these alloys.
